visa_types.name: Visiting Family / Friends Visa
visa_types.visa_fees:
Visa Fees for Hungary (Algeria)

[Tourism]

- Note: Nationals of Russia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Serbia, Montenegro, North Macedonia, and Bosnia and Herzegovina applying for short-stay visas are subject to a reduced fee of 35 EUR (5,200 DZD).
- Service Fee:
 
1,400 DZD (including VAT)
Optional Value-Added Services (VAS)

[Tourism 2]
All optional VAS services are available upon request and subject to availability.
Please Note:
The applicable visa fee in Algerian Dinar (DZD) is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
Applicants exempted from visa fee need to pay only Service Charge.
visa_types.processing_time:
The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days.
Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.
visa_types.forms:
This page hosts the form required for your Visa Application. Please click on the relevant form to download your visa application form.
Visa application form, please Click here
visa_types.overview:
Overview
For those visiting relatives or friends legally residing in Hungary. The host must provide an official invitation letter certified by Hungarian authorities. The applicant must prove their relationship with the host and provide accommodation details.
If you are planning a short visit to Hungary for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Hungary is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For those visiting relatives or friends legally residing in Hungary. The host must provide an official invitation letter certified by Hungarian authorities. The applicant must prove their relationship with the host and provide accommodation details.
If you are planning a short visit to Hungary for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Hungary is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees for Hungary (Algeria)

[Tourism]

- Note: Nationals of Russia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Serbia, Montenegro, North Macedonia, and Bosnia and Herzegovina applying for short-stay visas are subject to a reduced fee of 35 EUR (5,200 DZD).
- Service Fee:
 
1,400 DZD (including VAT)
Optional Value-Added Services (VAS)

[Tourism 2]
All optional VAS services are available upon request and subject to availability.
Please Note:
The applicable visa fee in Algerian Dinar (DZD) is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
Applicants exempted from visa fee need to pay only Service Charge.

## Documents Required

## 
Please click here to download the documents checklist
The Schengen visa allows entry and free movement on the territory of the states covered by the Schengen Agreements (Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Norway, the Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Switzerland, and Sweden). The duration of the short-stay visa not exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days, except for the shorter duration mentioned on the vignette. Visas for stays exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days are not Schengen visas and are subject to special conditions (residence permits, family reunification, etc.)
The Hungarian Embassy in Algiers deals only with Schengen visa applications
The only destination / main destination is Hungary
Additional documents may be requested, if necessary. The Hungarian Embassy reserves the right to summon the applicants for an interview.
The submission of a complete dossier does not necessarily entail the issuance of the visa.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Quality

- The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases
- The photographs must:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera must be high quality colour and printed on photo-quality paper.

## Style and lighting
- The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted, and showing both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red eye

## Glasses and head covers
- If you wear glasses :
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Head coverings :
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.

## Expression and frame

## Processing Time
The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days.
Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.

## Download Forms
This page hosts the form required for your Visa Application. Please click on the relevant form to download your visa application form.
Visa application form, please Click here

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For professionals traveling to Hungary for business-related activities such as meetings, conferences, trade fairs, or training. The applicant needs an invitation from a Hungarian business entity and proof of employment from their home country.
If you are planning a short visit to Hungary for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Hungary is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For individuals seeking medical treatment in Hungary. Requires official confirmation from a Hungarian healthcare institution stating the type of treatment, estimated duration, and proof of sufficient financial means to cover medical expenses.
If you are planning a short visit to Hungary for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Hungary is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For individuals traveling to Hungary for leisure, sightseeing, or vacations. The applicant must provide proof of accommodation, financial means, and a return ticket. Travel insurance covering the entire stay is required.
If you are planning a short visit to Hungary for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Hungary is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Issued to applicants attending cultural, scientific, artistic, or religious events in Hungary. Requires an official invitation or proof of registration for the event, along with details of accommodation and financial means.
For athletes, coaches, or team staff participating in sports competitions, tournaments, or training camps in Hungary. The organizing sports club or federation must issue an invitation letter detailing the event.
For government officials, diplomats, or representatives attending official meetings, conferences, or intergovernmental events in Hungary. Requires an official invitation from Hungarian authorities.
If you are planning a short visit to Hungary for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Hungary is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Issued to applicants attending cultural, scientific, artistic, or religious events in Hungary. Requires an official invitation or proof of registration for the event, along with details of accommodation and financial means.
For athletes, coaches, or team staff participating in sports competitions, tournaments, or training camps in Hungary. The organizing sports club or federation must issue an invitation letter detailing the event.
For government officials, diplomats, or representatives attending official meetings, conferences, or intergovernmental events in Hungary. Requires an official invitation from Hungarian authorities.
If you are planning a short visit to Hungary for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Hungary is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees for Hungary (Algeria)

[Tourism]

- Note: Nationals of Russia, Albania, Ukraine, Moldova, Serbia, Montenegro, North Macedonia, and Bosnia and Herzegovina applying for short-stay visas are subject to a reduced fee of 35 EUR (5,200 DZD).
- Service Fee:
 
1,400 DZD (including VAT)
Optional Value-Added Services (VAS)

[Tourism 2]
All optional VAS services are available upon request and subject to availability.
Please Note:
The applicable visa fee in Algerian Dinar (DZD) is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
Applicants exempted from visa fee need to pay only Service Charge.

## Documents Required

## 
Please click here to download the documents checklist
The Schengen visa allows entry and free movement on the territory of the states covered by the Schengen Agreements (Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Norway, the Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Switzerland, and Sweden). The duration of the short-stay visa not exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days, except for the shorter duration mentioned on the vignette. Visas for stays exceeding 90 days over a period of 180 days are not Schengen visas and are subject to special conditions (residence permits, family reunification, etc.)
The Hungarian Embassy in Algiers deals only with Schengen visa applications
The only destination / main destination is Hungary
Additional documents may be requested, if necessary. The Hungarian Embassy reserves the right to summon the applicants for an interview.
The submission of a complete dossier does not necessarily entail the issuance of the visa.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Quality

- The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases
- The photographs must:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera must be high quality colour and printed on photo-quality paper.

## Style and lighting
- The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted, and showing both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red eye

## Glasses and head covers
- If you wear glasses :
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Head coverings :
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.

## Expression and frame

## Processing Time
The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days.
Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.

## Download Forms
This page hosts the form required for your Visa Application. Please click on the relevant form to download your visa application form.
